Arulmigu Kasi Viswanathar Temple
Located in Tenkasi, which means “Southern Kashi,” this temple was built in the 15th century by Parakrama Pandyan of the Pandya dynasty.
The king established this temple as a southern equivalent to Kashi Vishwanath Temple so devotees could receive the blessings of Kashi without traveling to Varanasi.
The presiding deity is Lord Shiva as Kasi Vishwanathar (Lord of the Universe), and Goddess Parvati as Ulagamman, meaning Mother of the World.
The temple features a majestic 7-tier Rajagopuram with intricate carvings reflecting classic Pandya architecture.
It is believed that worship here gives spiritual merit similar to visiting Kashi.
Surrounded by the serene Western Ghats, the temple offers a peaceful and powerful spiritual atmosphere.

The Architectural 'Southern Kasi'

Historical Overview:
The history of Tenkasi ('Southern Kasi') is inseparable from this magnificent temple dedicated to Lord Shiva as Kasi Viswanathar. Founded by the devoted Pandyan ruler Parakrama Pandyan in the 15th century, the temple was conceptualized to serve as a spiritual equivalent to Varanasi in the north. This monumental undertaking ensured that devotees could receive the merits of a Kasi pilgrimage without traversing the entire subcontinent. The temple complex, an exceptional example of mature Dravidian architecture, features a sprawling layout and towering structures, establishing its profound cultural and religious significance as a hub of the late Pandya empire.

Review:
As a pilgrimage from this nation who seeks out those rare sites where engineering and divinity perfectly align, I must confirm that this is a truly mesmerizing location. For a first visit, the sheer scale is overwhelming, immediately setting it apart. The architecture, particularly the colossal, seven-tiered Rajagopuram, is an absolute masterpiece—a unique feature I bet you simply won't find replicated anywhere else in its artistry and execution.

What elevates the spiritual experience here is the atmosphere. We had a wonderfully calm and peaceful darshan, a rarity these days in major historic South Indian temples, making us feel genuinely blessed. Beyond the deity, one must pause to admire the incredible architectural intelligence on display. The way the Rajagopuram entrance manages air flow—generating two distinct, opposite drafts in the same passage, creating a subtle, vacuum-like buffer space right at the entry point—is a marvel of ancient Vastu and engineering. It's an ethereal experience that literally feels like the temple is breathing.

This temple is not just a place of worship; it's a structural genius's dream and a must-visit for anyone seeking peace, architectural inspiration, and a truly authentic and unhurried spiritual moment. I’m already eager to plan a return.

Critical Architectural Insight:
The famous, intricately sculpted 180-foot Rajagopuram, completed in 1990 after a historic fire, is a modern reconstruction that beautifully honors the classical Dravidian style. When you enter, pause directly beneath the towering arch. Feel the unique dual-stream airflow—this subtle, natural air conditioning effect is a testament to the brilliant, almost lost science of temple construction. Plan your visit during non-peak darshan hours (early morning or late afternoon) to fully appreciate the profound quietude and the architectural grandeur in comfort.

Arulmigu Kasi Viswanathar Temple, Tenkasi is one of the most prominent Shaiva temples in Tamil Nadu, located in the town of Tenkasi near the Western Ghats. The temple is dedicated to Lord Shiva worshipped as Kasi Viswanathar, with Goddess Ulagamman as his consort. It is popularly known as the “Kasi of the South,” as legend says that devotees who could not travel to Varanasi (Kasi) could worship here and receive the same spiritual merit.
The temple was built in the 15th century by King Parakrama Pandya of the Pandya dynasty. The most striking feature of the temple is its majestic nine-tiered Rajagopuram, rising to a height of about 180 feet, making it one of the tallest temple towers in Tamil Nadu. The gopuram is richly adorned with detailed sculptures depicting scenes from Hindu mythology.
According to legend, a sacred Shiva Lingam from Kasi was installed here after divine intervention, leading to the name Tenkasi (Southern Kasi). The temple is also closely associated with the holy Chittra Sabhai and sacred theerthams.
Major festivals such as Masi Magam, Aadi Thapasu, and Shivaratri are celebrated with great devotion. The temple stands as a fine blend of spiritual significance, history, and Dravidian architectural grandeur.
Great Architectural n engineering marvel of this Rajagopuram is that if devotees who enter the temple are pushed to the inside of the temple by the air. At the same time if devotees who are coming out are being pushed out side by the air at the same time.  This is the power of the temple

Thirumalai Kumaraswamy Temple, Panpoli – The Breathtaking Hilltop Retreat

Historical Significance (The Hilltop Murugan):
The Arulmigu Thirumalai Kumaraswamy Temple, a sacred hilltop shrine nestled in Panpoli near Sengottai in the Tenkasi district, is a remarkable example of Dravidian hill architecture dedicated to Lord Murugan. Situated at an elevation of around 330 meters in the lush Western Ghats, this temple is revered as a Vaippu Sthalam, praised in ancient Tamil hymns, and holds special significance for devotees born under the Vishakha Nakshatra. Legend connects the site to the great Sage Agasthiar, who is said to have received darshan from Lord Murugan here, adding profound spiritual depth to its stunning natural location. The temple has long been a major pilgrimage site, but thanks to its recent popularity boost, it's now firmly on the global traveller's map.

The Global Reviewer's Experience:
As a reviewer who judges destinations on their ability to offer both spiritual enlightenment and visual spectacle, I can confirm: this spot is truly mesmerizing, and I'm already eager to plan a return!

My journey was a simple detour while en route to Courtallam and Tenkasi, yet this temple stole the show. It’s gained massive recognition recently—and rightly so—after being used as a shooting location for the globally famous Pushpa movie (and I'm sure it'll be prominent in Pushpa 2). While the cinematic connection was the initial pull, the two surprises that followed are what made the trip unforgettable.

First, the realization that this beautiful hill has been a backdrop for numerous Tamil movies over the years. You can practically feel the cinematic history in the air! Second, and most beautiful, was the sheer ambience. Wow! I can still feel the chillness of the breeze now. Surrounded by the majestic Eastern Ghats, with the dam glittering below and endless stretches of green rice fields blanketing the valley, the views are picture-perfect at every turn. We were lucky enough to even catch a glorious rainbow arcing across the hilltop—a moment of pure, divine magic.

The road winding up the mountain, though small, felt like a scenic tour itself, offering one stunning vista after another. We absolutely revealed in the refreshing breeze, the panoramic views, and the sacred atmosphere. Unfortunately, because we visited in the afternoon, the temple was closed, and we missed the darshan. But honestly, the pilgrimage was complete just by experiencing the surroundings.

A Note on Cleanliness and Responsibility:
I must address one truly disheartening sight. It was truly horrible to see so many discarded plastic covers, bottles, food waste, and even slippers dumped, making parts of this beautiful, sacred spot resemble a dumpyard. We need to be mindful when visiting common areas, particularly a revered pilgrimage site like this. Did we keep garbage around our own deity? Let’s exercise a basic sense of responsibility before pointing fingers at the government or temple administration—the root cause of this mismanagement is often the visitors themselves.

Final Verdict:
I strongly recommend this temple to all travellers jamming the famous Courtallam falls: please carve out some time to visit Thirumalai. You’ll find a level of peace, beauty, and history here that makes it an essential stop on any South Indian itinerary. We'll definitely be back for that darshan!

Wonderful waterfall... Worth to visit...  Palaruvi Waterfalls – The Stream of Milk in the Western Ghats

Nestled gracefully in the lush folds of the Western Ghats near Aryankavu in Kerala’s Kollam district, Palaruvi Waterfalls is one of the most enchanting natural treasures of South India.  the water cascading down from a height of about 300 feet, you will understand why. The white, frothy water tumbles down the rocky cliff like flowing milk, creating a breathtaking spectacle that captivates every visitor’s heart.

Surrounded by dense tropical forests, misty hills, and the soothing sounds of chirping birds, Palaruvi offers an escape into pure serenity. The journey to the waterfall itself is magical. As you travel through winding roads bordered by rubber plantations and evergreen trees, the cool breeze carries the earthy scent of the forest. The anticipation builds with every step closer to the roaring sound of falling water.

When you finally reach the waterfall, the sight is both powerful and calming. The water crashes onto rocks below, forming a refreshing pool where visitors can enjoy a natural bath. The area is well-maintained and safe for families, making it a popular destination for tourists, nature lovers, photographers, and adventure seekers alike. The cool mountain water is believed to have medicinal properties due to the herbs and roots it flows over in the forest, adding to its charm and significance.

Palaruvi is not just about the waterfall; it is about the entire experience. The surrounding forest is rich in biodiversity, home to various species of plants, butterflies, and birds. During the monsoon season, the waterfall becomes even more majestic, with increased water flow creating a thunderous yet mesmerizing display of nature’s strength. In contrast, the post-monsoon and winter months offer a gentler flow, ideal for bathing and relaxing.

The atmosphere around Palaruvi is filled with peace and positivity. The mist rising from the falls creates a dreamlike aura, especially when sunlight filters through the trees, forming tiny rainbows in the spray. It is a place where one can forget the noise of the world and reconnect with nature’s simplicity. Whether you are standing close to feel the splash on your face or sitting quietly on a rock listening to the rhythm of the water, every moment spent here feels rejuvenating.

Palaruvi Waterfalls is more than just a tourist attraction—it is a reminder of nature’s raw beauty and timeless grace. A visit here is not just a trip; it is a refreshing journey for the soul, leaving behind memories as pure and beautiful as the waterfall itself.... Don't forget to visit....

An Architectural Marvel: Beyond Words"
"Visiting the Arulmigu sankaranarayana swamy temple  was an experience that truly defies description. It is not just a temple; it is an architectural marvel carved into stone.
The intricate stone carvings and sculptures are simply astounding—a visual wonder that leaves you speechless. To think of the immense effort and dedication required to construct such a masterpiece over 1,000 years ago is mind-boggling. The sheer scale and detail are beyond magnificent.
Stepping inside the temple premises is like entering another world altogether. The profound atmosphere instantly transports you, creating an almost surreal and intensely spiritual feeling.
In short: Spectacular, breathtaking, magnificent! This temple is an absolute must-see, a place that embodies the greatness of our ancient heritage."

Very huge, ancient and beautiful temple.  Big beautiful gopuram. There are three separate shrines for three main deities- Shankaralingam, Shankaranarayanan and Gomati ammal.  Besides these, there are shrines for Varaahi, Valli devyani sametha Subramanya Swami and Nataraja.

There was also this sweet looking temple elephant we were lucky and happy to see.

However I sincerely feel that the temple and it's surroundings can be more cleanly maintained and the devotees offering prasad to the public also take care of proper waste disposal and marked areas for washing of hands after partaking the prasad they bring.  We did not find any taps to wash our hands.  A stall vendor offered her water bottle and we washed out hands with very little water in a common area.  Because it was raining, it wasn't noticed or very evident.  But still I felt bad as there was no other area with taps for devotees to wash their hands.

If you plan to buy flowers garlands, please buy three atleast for the three main deities.  I had taken just one garland and some bananas as I didn't know there were three deities.  So I gave the garland at the Shankaralingam altar and the bananas at the Shankaranarayanan altar.  I didn't have anything to give at the Gomati ammal altar.  Please don't make this mistake.

Vibrant, peaceful, soothing, divine temple.  Good maintenance. Elephant's presence adds awesome vibes. Sankara lingam, Shankaranarayanan, Gomati ammal, pulithevar gugai,  sree yenthram, spadiga lingam...all are real blessing to experience. Must see temple.

Tiger Falls is very near to main falls and the water is always coming same level, so it won't be closed even in heavy rain. You will get a good experience when you put your head and body into the falls, great therapy. Enjoy the moment.

Tiger Falls is often talked about as a tourist spot, but in reality, it’s quite a small waterfall. You’ll see just a stream of water trickling down from the rocks rather than a powerful fall. If you go with the expectation of a big scenic waterfall, you might feel underwhelmed.
One of the biggest downsides is the crowd. On weekends and holidays, the place becomes extremely busy, and the charm of sitting peacefully by the falls is lost in the rush. Parking and vehicle entry are not managed well, which makes the whole experience tiring even before reaching the spot.
There are a few small shops nearby selling snacks and refreshments, which is convenient if you’re spending some time there. However, the lack of proper facilities and organization makes the place feel a bit chaotic.
Overall, Tiger Falls is fine for a short stop if you’re passing through or if you just want to say you’ve been there. But if you’re looking for a proper waterfall experience, this one may not live up to expectations.

Extremely picturesque place, really good atmosphere. Gets kind of sunny but if you love walking a lot, you'd love it. The views are aesthetic and refreshing and from the top, it's amazing. The vastness of the amount of water in the reservoir really hits you from the top. Lots of monkeys, make sure you hold your food in your hand tight.

Visited Thenmala recently and it was a really beautiful and refreshing experience. The dam area is scenic and peaceful, perfect to just slow down and enjoy nature. We also did the forest safari and visited the deer rehabilitation center — it was nice to see the place being well maintained and calm, especially surrounded by greenery.

The coracle ride was my favorite part. The river was so calm and beautiful, with a very soothing vibe. Overall, Thenmala is a perfect destination if you want a mix of nature, light adventure, and peace. A must-visit place for a relaxed day trip.

Thenmala Dam is honestly one of the best dam sites I have ever seen. The view is simply breathtaking. When the sunlight reflects on the water, it looks absolutely mesmerizing, something that’s really hard to put into words. You just stand there and take it all in.
The wind here is quite strong and incredibly refreshing, which makes the whole experience even better. The surroundings feel calm, peaceful, and close to nature, making it a perfect place to relax and unwind.
Overall, this place is totally worth visiting. If you enjoy scenic views, fresh air, and serene natural beauty, Thenmala Dam is a must see.

Thenmala Dam is a beautiful sight, especially during the monsoon when the reservoir is full. Unlike some other dams in Kerala, there are many spots here where you can actually take great photos. The Leisure Zone and the Suspension Bridge offer fantastic views of the structure and the river. It’s a peaceful place for a walk, and the musical fountain in the evening is a must-see. Highly recommend for families!
If you go near Thenmala, you must visit the dam barrage; the entry fee is only ₹10."

A good place to spend a day with friends or family. You can visit the power generating station by getting prior permission. Good parking area. Beware of monkeys. Children's park is available. Tickets are available at the entrance. Worth a visit. Usually not crowded. Bringing snacks is recommended.

My first trek with Indiahikes to Kedarkantha was an incredible experience and a great introduction to Himalayan trekking. The entire trek was very well organized — from clear communication before the trek to the on-ground execution by the trek leaders and support staff.

What stood out most was the professionalism and safety focus of the team. The trek leaders were knowledgeable, approachable, and made sure everyone in the group was comfortable and acclimatizing well. The campsites were beautiful, the food on the trek was surprisingly good, and the overall management made the journey enjoyable rather than stressful.

Kedarkantha itself is a fantastic first Himalayan trek with stunning views throughout, and doing it with Indiahikes made the experience smooth and memorable. I’d definitely recommend them to anyone looking to start their trekking journey in the Himalayas.

I recently completed the Dayara Bugyal Himalayan trek with Indiahikes, and it turned out to be one of the finest trekking experiences I have had so far.
The entire trek was very well organized, and I would like to specially acknowledge the excellent work of our Trek Leader, Ms. Monica, along with our guides Jaydeep and Narendra. Ms. Monica was truly outstanding in her role. She was extremely supportive, approachable, and knowledgeable. Throughout the trek, she explained in detail about the mountains, forest cover, trees, trail conditions, safety precautions, and the essential do’s and don’ts of trekking. Her clear instructions at every step ensured safety, discipline, and confidence among all trekkers.
The logistics, including accommodation and trekking equipment, were well managed and met high standards. Having completed more than 9–10 treks earlier, I can confidently say that this trek stands out as one of my best experiences. The credit for this goes entirely to Indiahikes and its dedicated, professional team.
As constructive feedback, I feel that the food quality and quantity can be improved further to make the trek experience absolutely perfect.
Overall, the Dayara Bugyal trek was memorable, enriching, and extremely well conducted. I highly recommend Indiahikes to anyone looking for a safe, educational, and well-organized Himalayan trekking experience.
-DR.KARTIK BHADRA, VALSAD,GUJARAT

I recently did the Chhattisgarh jungle trek and never know how much went into planning a seamless trek and after my first trek I have to say I’m extremely glad I chose Indiahikes. They clearly know what they are doing and made us feel so amazing throughout. The coordination, nutrition and accommodation ( both physical and mental ) was truly outstanding. I was so excited and sad at the same time when the trek was over that I booked the next one right away. Highly, highly recommend trekking with Indiahikes. This trek had a lot of firsts for me personally and if you are ready to explore and deal with slight discomforts you will be rewarded multifold.

Kudos to him and the kitchen team who worked tirelessly, can’t thank them enough. Go on an adventure with Indiahikes without second thoughts, I’m sure it will be life changing.

An unforgettable mountain experience! The trek was beautifully organized with a very supportive and professional staff throughout the journey. They ensured everyone felt comfortable and confident at every stage.

The food was surprisingly good for such cold high-altitude conditions, and the team took great care of us at every campsite. Their encouragement and planning made the summit day smooth and memorable.
Overall, a superb experience from start to finish.
Highly recommended to anyone planning the Kedarkantha Winter Trek!

Thalaiyanai waterfalls is inside Kalakad Mundanthurai Tiger Reserve (KMTR). “Thalaiyanai” in Tamil means pillow, The rock formation at the falls resembles a giant stone pillow. Entry to the waterfalls area is regulated by Forest department at the entrance/check post. They check for disposable plastic items/carry bags/water bottles. Entry ticket needs to be purchased at the counter within 100 meters. Adults Rs. 40 and Kids Rs.30

Hidden Gem Near Coutralam Falls! 🌿💦

Wow! What a lovely and beautiful place to enjoy. Located just 40 km away from Coutralam Falls, this mesmerizing spot is far less crowded, making it a peaceful escape.

Though the waterfall here is smaller in height, the force of the water is incredible—you literally can’t stand against it! It pushes you right off your spot, which makes it super fun, especially when you're with a group of friends. 🎉

Right next to the falls, you’ll find a dam, which is quite rare to witness together. I’ve never seen a dam and waterfall side by side like this—it’s truly a unique experience!

They’ve thoughtfully provided separate dress changing rooms for ladies, ensuring privacy and safety. 👍

The same road also leads to Manjolai, which is a restricted forest area where entry is not allowed without special permission.

This entire place is under the control of the Forest Department, and there are strict security checks. Carrying snacks, food, or drinks is not allowed—if you bring any, they’ll be taken at the check post and returned to you on the way back.

📅 Best time to visit: June to August, when the falls are at their best.

At a distance of 27 km from Tenkasi, the Karuppanadhi Dam is an earthen dam located at Chokkampatti, in the Tirunelveli district of Tamil Nadu. It is one of the important dams in Tamilnadu, and among the top tourist places near Tenkasi.

Located at the foothills of Western Ghats, the Karuppanadhi Dam was built across the Karuppanadhi River. The river Karuppanathi is a branch of Tamirabarani. It originates at an altitude of 5870 feet falls in the Krishnapuram village of Kadayanallur taluk, in Tamil Nadu. The dam construction was started in 1971 and was completed in the year of 1977.

The dam is 34m in height above the lowest foundation, and the length of the dam is 899 m. The volume content of the dam is about 10 million cubics. It has two sluice gates. It provides water for irrigation to the region of Kadayanallur Taluk. The best time to visit the dam is during the monsoon season when the dam is full and the gates are opened which is a sight to behold.

I had a nice experience visiting Thenmala with my brother. We selected the leisure ticket, which allows you to walk around the park. Inside, you can see monkeys, flowers, wild squirrels, sculptures, and views of the Kallada Dam in the area.
There are many packages available in Thenmala, so make sure to check before you go. Activities like trekking, adventure activities, and boating are also available. It’s a really nice place to visit and enjoy nature.

Spent the day at the Thenmala Leisure Zone and it was a great experience. The entire place has such a serene beauty that really helps your mind feel calm. I loved the walk through the trees; they’ve placed benches quite thoughtfully so you can actually sit back and enjoy the silence. There’s plenty of wildlife too—lots of birds, butterflies, and the monkeys near the Thookkupalam made the visit pretty interesting. Visiting Thenmala Ecotourism was an unforgettable experience — truly one of the finest places I have visited in Kerala. Nestled amidst the lush greenery of the Western Ghats, the setting feels calm, serene and beautifully natural. The air is fresh, the surroundings silent, and you immediately sense how far removed you are from city life.

The scenic beauty of the forest, the tranquil waters of the reservoir, and the harmonious blend of nature and thoughtful eco-tourism make Thenmala a perfect escape. There are plenty of adventures on offer if you choose — from gentle treks and boating to exploring forest trails and wildlife zones — yet there are also quiet corners where you can simply sit back and soak in the peace.

Whether you are a nature lover, an adventure seeker or someone just longing for rest and quiet, Thenmala delivers. It’s a place where nature’s beauty and tranquility meet thoughtful design — ideal for a rejuvenating getaway.

Highly recommended for anyone looking to unwind, reconnect with nature and enjoy a beautifully calm retreat.

A good destination to plan a day out with family. It has multiple facilities to keep us engaged for a full day. They have a car parking facility which can accommodate few cars. However parking would not be problem as cars can parked even by the roadside. The ticket rates are reasonable, and they accept UPI transactions. They have a children's park and butterfly park, which would be of interest to the children. A toy train is also available. The deer rehabilitation centre and leisure zone with dam view provides a nature walk. Few activities including boating, roap climbing, shooting, roap way, are available in the adventure zone.

Few shops are available near the ticket counter which sell essentials like water, snacks etc. A restaurant is also available inside the campus.

Though enormous area is available, a serious lack of proper maintenance or adequate upgradation was felt.

Overall it was a very refreshing experience.

Thenmala Ecotourism offers a variety of activities for visitors. There is an entry fee, and each activity requires an additional charge. At the ticket counter, we were informed that the butterfly park currently has very few butterflies, so we decided to skip it. We then visited the children’s park, which has a toy train for kids available for an extra fee. Unfortunately, the park was not well maintained — many sculptures were damaged, the paint was fading, and several installations were in poor condition.

The musical fountain, however, was pleasant and stood out as one of the better attractions. Overall, the place has great potential but would benefit significantly from better maintenance and upkeep.

The view from the top is absolutely stunning, featuring lush green paddy fields and a magical sunset in the evening. It is a wonderfully peaceful, uncrowded spot, perfect for relaxing and enjoying the incredibly strong and refreshing wind. The site also features a unique touch, a few large rocks painted with the faces of Tamil film actors, which felt like a charming mini-tribute to Kollywood. Overall, it’s a great spot for anyone who loves nature, movies, and a calm vibe.

Anniyan Para View Point is a beautiful and peaceful place with a stunning view of the surrounding paddy fields. The scenery is calm and refreshing, making it a great spot for nature lovers and photography. The viewpoint offers a wide, open landscape that feels untouched and serene.

However, visitors should be aware that there are no shops or facilities nearby, so it is important to carry enough drinking water and essentials in advance. Also, unfortunately, broken liquor bottles are scattered in some areas, which can be dangerous. Please be careful while walking, especially if you are visiting with children.

Overall, it is a lovely place to enjoy nature, but visitors should come prepared and be cautious to ensure a safe and pleasant experience.

The Gundar Dam is a reservoir located at the foothills of the Western Ghats, near Courtallam and Tenkasi in the Tenkasi district. It is also an important tourist destination in the Tenkasi district. This reservoir was constructed in 1983 to meet the irrigation needs of the then Tirunelveli district. Gundar is a natural reservoir. The length of this reservoir is 389 meters, and its height is 36.10 meters. This reservoir is a medium-scale irrigation project. The construction work of this reservoir began in 1979 and was completed in 1983. The capacity of this reservoir is 25 million cubic feet.

Rajathottam View Point is an absolutely stunning location tucked away in the hills near Thenmala. The view from the top is surreal — endless green valleys, mist-covered peaks, and the towns below look like tiny dots. The silence here is something rare… just the wind, rustling grass, and the occasional call of a bird.

The road leading up is narrow and a bit rough, but totally worth it if you love offbeat nature spots. On the way, you pass clove plantations and thick forest trails. The final stretch requires a short hike (nothing too difficult), but the reward is one of the best panoramic views I’ve seen in South India.

Tip: Try to visit early in the morning or just before sunset. Also, carry water and snacks — there aren’t any shops nearby. Locals are friendly and may even offer guidance if you ask politely.

Located between Tamil Nadu and Kerala, Manalar Waterfalls is a seasonal, slow-moving waterfall in the Western Ghats. It is also known as Achankovil Falls. The Thirumalai Kumaraswamy Temple, popularly known as Thirumalai Kovil, is a breathtaking hill temple located in Panpoli, near Sengottai. It is perched on a small hill (about 400 meters high) at the foot of the Western Ghats, near the Kerala border.
🏛️ The Divine Origin (Sthala Puranam)
According to legend, about 600 years ago, the presiding deity, Thirumalai Muthu Kumaraswamy, appeared in a dream to a priest named Poovan Battar.
The Message: Lord Murugan told the priest that his idol was buried under a bamboo bush in a place called Kottaithiradu.
The Sign: He was told that a line of ants would lead him to the exact spot.
The Discovery: The priest informed the King of Pandalam, and together they followed the ants, found the idol, and installed it at the current hilltop location.
The Scar: During the excavation, the idol’s nose was slightly damaged. To this day, you can see a small scar on the deity's nose. Because of this, it is a local tradition to name children Mookkan or Mookkamma (referring to the nose).
Feature Details
Steps 625 steps lead to the top (offering great views of coconut groves).
Accessibility There is now a motorable road for those who cannot climb.
Architecture Classic Dravidian style; the hill is said to be shaped like the "Om" symbol.
Distance ~5 km from Panpoli, ~8 km from Sengottai, and ~20 km from Tenkasi.

Nestled amidst breathtaking nature, this temple offers a truly serene and spiritual experience that captivates all who visit. Surrounded by lush greenery and vibrant flowers, the scenic beauty enhances the temple's peaceful atmosphere, making it an ideal place for reflection and meditation. Visitors will find ample parking space, ensuring convenience for those traveling by vehicle. For those who prefer a more traditional approach, there are steps leading up to the temple, allowing for a contemplative journey to the sacred site. Additionally, a small fee permits close vehicle access, making it accessible for everyone, regardless of their preference. Whether you choose to walk or drive, the harmony of nature and the tranquil ambiance of the temple promise a fulfilling experience. It’s a perfect escape from the hustle and bustle of daily life, where one can connect spiritually and find solace in the beauty that surrounds them.

Tirumalai Kovil is a beautiful and peaceful temple located in Tenkasi, The temple's location, perched beautifully on a hill, provides a spectacular vista and contributes to the truly very good ambience that permeates the entire area.

Be prepared that accessing the main sanctum involves climbing so many steps. It is a significant walk, so if you decide to take the stairs, remember to keep some water in hand to stay hydrated during the ascent. However, the spiritual reward and the sense of accomplishment once you reach the top are profound.

For convenience, the temple offers bus transportation from down to top, which will cost you ₹50. This is a great option as you can enter the temple skipping the steps and saving energy, especially for those traveling with family or with mobility concerns.

To fully enjoy the serenity and avoid the crowds and heat, I strongly suggest you must visit before 7-8 AM; this is a genuinely good time for darshan. Once you are at the summit, there is a small shop available, which is handy for refreshments or purchasing flowers for the deity.

This is truly a must-visit temple for its spiritual significance and breathtaking hilltop setting

Perched quietly in the lush folds of the Western Ghats, the Thirumalai Murugan Temple—popularly called Thirumalai Kovil—offers a beautiful blend of devotion, nature, and legend. Overlooking the village of Panpoli near Sengottai in Tamil Nadu’s Tenkasi district, this hill temple feels less like a tourist spot and more like a timeless spiritual retreat suspended between earth and sky.

The journey to the shrine itself is an experience to cherish. Devotees either take on the enriching climb of over 600 steps or choose the scenic motorable road that winds gently up the hill. At around 400 meters above sea level, the views slowly unfold into misty greens, making the ascent as meditative as the darshan itself.

At the heart of the temple stands Lord Murugan, worshipped here as Thirumalai Kumaraswamy. The deity is seen in a majestic standing posture with four hands, radiating strength and protection. Alongside Murugan, the presence of Thirumalai Kali Amman adds a powerful Shakti element to the sacred complex, balancing energy and grace.

The temple’s origins are steeped in divine legend. It is believed that the Lord revealed Himself in a dream to a priest, leading to the discovery of the idol and the eventual consecration of the temple under the patronage of the Pandalam ruler of Kerala. Though its present structure dates back to around the 15th century, the hill itself has far older roots—it is even mentioned in the ancient Tamil epic Silappatikaram as “Neduvelkundram,” highlighting its long-standing spiritual importance.

One of the most enchanting features of this hill shrine is the sacred water body known as Ashta Padmakulam or Poonjunai, said to have been formed by Sage Agasthiyar. Adding to the mysticism, the hill is believed to resemble the sacred syllable “OM,” reinforcing its status as a powerful center of divine vibration.

Thirumalai Murugan Temple is not just a place of worship—it is a serene sanctuary where mythology, nature, and faith merge effortlessly. Whether you come seeking blessings, inner peace, or a quiet moment amid mountains and mist, this sacred hill leaves you with a deep sense of calm and devotion long after you descend.

We have trekked to the Shenbaga Devi waterfalls booking through TrekTamilnadu website. It was a paid trek guided by Tamilnadu Forest Dept. Guides. The whole trek was an amazing experience.
It’s a 2+2=4 km trek, where we climbed through the laid steps and rocks with the water flowing along-side from every possible passage. The sounds of the chirping birds and the flowing water was so soothing throughout the journey. The water was so clear and tasted so fresh and pure, Bliss!
Lush green denser trees canopied the entire path. The air had the scent of Mother Nature and that breezed around its chill embrace. A serenity was set on my mind, that expanded my heart to fall in love with the very moment.
Eventually, we had reached the Shenbaga Devi Temple, paid a brief salutation to the Devi from outside, as the temple was closed at that time.
As we walked forward, behind the temple stood the majestic beauty of the Shenbaga Devi waterfall. The view was so stunning 🤩. I had kept my eyes glued to the flowing water for nearly a minute. If you are looking for a place to find true inner peace, Sivagiri Mutt is a destination you must visit at least once. It is a very nice place with a serene environment that feels incredibly calm and refreshing. The entire premises are maintained with high standards of cleanliness, which adds to the overall positive energy of the site.
The most unique and beautiful feature of the Mutt is the abundance of wisdom quotes written and hanging in almost every corner. You should definitely take the time to read them; they offer profound life lessons and provide a very peaceful mind. Varkala is famous because of this sacred Mutt, which serves as the final resting place of the great social reformer Sree Narayana Guru.
Practicality is also a plus here, as there is lots of parking space available, making it very convenient for families and pilgrims traveling by car. Whether you are coming for a spiritual pilgrimage or just a quiet break from the hustle and bustle of the beach, Sivagiri Mutt offers a soulful experience that stays with you long after you leave.

Sivagiri Mutt is a serene and spiritually inspiring destination. The premises are tidy, tranquil, and ideal for moments of reflection. The memorial of Sree Narayana Guru is well-kept, and the ambience feels truly divine. There is sufficient parking and the overall facilities are good. A highly recommended spot in Varkala for those interested in heritage, culture, and spiritual experiences.
Every corner radiates tranquility, with clean pathways and an inclusive vibe that rejuvenates the soul. This place will leave you refreshed and deeply at peace. A must-visit gem in Varkala.

Kuthara Paanjan Waterfalls (also called Kuthirapanjan or Panagudi Falls) is a nice, hidden spot in the green hills near Panagudi, Tirunelveli, Tamil Nadu. It has beautiful nature, a cool pool to swim in, and is fun for families with kids. Many people give it 5 out of 5. The water flows well after rains, especially December to January (like now in late January 2026, it should be okay if rains were good). But in summer (Feb–June), there's little or no water. Weekends get crowded, and some visitors leave trash, so it can get dirty. You need to trek a short way through forest paths. Overall, it's a peaceful, refreshing place—go early, don't litter, and enjoy the views and dip! 😊

Kuthirapanjan Waterfalls, also known as Panagudi Kuthirapanjan Waterfalls, is a scenic natural attraction located in the Western Ghats, near Panagudi in the Thoothukudi district of Tamil Nadu, India. Situated amidst lush mountain ranges and the Mahendragiri hills, these falls are approximately 55 km from Tirunelveli. The waterfall cascades from a height of about 150 feet, creating a captivating view. Visitors can enjoy the beautiful scenery and clear water, with opportunities for photography. The area is also suitable for adventurous activities like rock climbing, rope climbing, and camping. To reach the falls, visitors typically trek about 2 km through a well-laid path within thick forests from the parking area. The nearest airport is Tirunelveli, approximately 95 km away.

🌊 Kuthara Paanjan Waterfalls – Beauty with a Bear Warning! 🐻😂

One random morning, we friends went on a trip to Kuthara Paanjan Waterfalls thinking it’ll be peaceful and cool. And yes – the falls were super pretty, water was fresh, and the spot felt like a hidden gem. 💦🌿

But then… a local uncle came and said:

“Be careful, a bear might come give you a hug.” 😳

Excuse me, what kind of welcome is that?! 😂
We thought he was joking, but we lowkey got scared and started walking around like spies in the forest, listening to every sound. 🫣

Anyway, no bear showed up (thank god), and the place was awesome!

Go early, enjoy the view, but maybe don’t stay for the “hug.” 🐾😅

The "Thenmala Dam Bridge" is a term that can refer to the suspension bridge near the Thenmala Dam, which is located in the Kollam district of Kerala, India. This suspension bridge is a popular feature of the Thenmala ecotourism destination and offers visitors views of the dam and the Kallada River, which flows under it. Visitors can experience attractions like boating on the dam's lake, trekking, and a musical fountain nearby.

Location: Near Thenmala Dam, which is in the foothills of the Western Ghats in Kerala's Kollam district.
Main attractions: Provides scenic views of the dam and the Kallada River.

Activities: Part of the larger Thenmala ecotourism spot, which includes boating, trekking, a rope bridge, and a musical fountain.

Dam information: The Thenmala Dam is also known as the Kallada Dam or Parappara Dam. It is a key feature of the area, which is India's first planned ecotourism destination.

Eco Park Courtallam is a prominent eco-park situated near the renowned Five Falls in Courtallam, Tamil Nadu. Established over 37 acres, it features attractions such as an adventure play area, sunken garden, fern garden, royal drive, bamboo avenue, rock garden, children's play area, murals, butterfly garden, eco pond, and stream walk. We recently visited Manalar Falls, and let me tell you – it's not our typical touristy falls like we see in Tamil Nadu. The Kerala forest staff here are very strict and thoroughly check every visitor before allowing entry, which gives a good sense of safety and responsibility.

Though there's an entry fee of ₹50, the crowd was still massive, with people of all age groups – from kids to elders – walking in to enjoy the natural beauty.

Ticket counter will open on 8.30am only
We took tickets as the 3rd group for the day and rushed ahead to beat the crowd. The 750m walk wasn't easy – it's not a plain route. There were small ups and downs and the ground was quite slippery and mossy in places, especially since it had rained lightly.

But the early entry was worth it! We got to enjoy the falls before it got crowded.

What makes Manalar Falls unique is its multi-level fall system:

Water first falls into a small natural pool (like a mini lake).

Then it flows down again into another layer.

Then again into a third stream section.

You can actually take a dip at all 3 levels – and each one was absolutely amazing!

Important Note:
There are a lot of leeches ("atta poochi") here – even inside the water. So be very careful while bathing, and after finishing your dip, make sure to thoroughly check yourself and your clothes.

Perfect for early birds who want to beat the crowd and experience raw, natural beauty.

The place is very serene and offers a peaceful escape. The trek to reach the falls is short but enjoyable, making it a great spot for relaxing. The calming natural surroundings and flowing water create a perfect setting to unwind.

However, the experience is somewhat spoiled by visitors using the waterfalls for bathing, which isn't ideal given the small size of the falls. When many people are there, it becomes crowded, making it difficult to enjoy the tranquility. Some visitors, including men with larger physiques and taller individuals, tend to occupy the space for extended periods. Additionally, older visitors and women above 40 may find it challenging to access the falls comfortably.

The falls have two steps; the lower one is often less crowded, and some might avoid bathing there due to concerns about hygiene. It’s advisable not to carry plastics or alcohol inside, as I noticed a vodka bottle hidden in the woods despite security. There’s no designated space for changing clothes, so plan accordingly. Parking is roadside, with charges for cars, adults, and children.

Overall, it’s a lovely spot to visit with family, enjoy the cool water, and listen to the sounds of nature. Just relax, wash your legs, and soak in the peaceful environment. It’s definitely worth the drive. Remember, water bodies like these should be enjoyed respectfully, without misusing the divine surroundings.

Adventure and Scenic Beauty Combined

Amappara is a destination that perfectly blends thrill with natural charm. The off‑road jeep safari from Thovalappady junction sets the tone for adventure, with rugged paths leading you deep into the hills. Crawling through the narrow natural rock crevice adds a unique, exciting twist that makes the visit unforgettable.

Once you reach the top, the views are simply breathtaking, vast landscapes, rocky formations, and a serene atmosphere that feels untouched and pure. Visiting in the evening for the sunset is highly recommended, as the golden light over the hills creates a magical scene.

Overall, Amappara is a must‑visit for adventure seekers and nature lovers alike, a place where thrill and tranquility meet in perfect harmony.

Visited Amappara, Ramakkalmedu in the evening, and it turned out to be one of the most memorable travel experiences in Idukki.

We first took the jeep safari to reach the top, which itself was an exciting adventure. The off-road ride through the hills, greenery, and narrow mountain paths added a lot of thrill to the journey and made the experience even more special. The cool breeze and scenic views during the ride were refreshing.

Once we reached the top, the viewpoint opened up to a breathtaking 360-degree panorama of hills, valleys, windmills, and distant plains. The evening atmosphere was magical, and the weather was perfect. The highlight was the sunset view — the sky slowly turning orange, pink, and purple as the sun disappeared behind the mountains created a picture-perfect moment.

One of the main attractions here is the natural tortoise-shaped rock cave.
Reaching the cave and trying to enter inside is a real adventurous experience. The entry is quite difficult and narrow, but once inside, it feels like discovering a hidden natural wonder. It’s not for the faint-hearted, but definitely worth the effort for adventure lovers.

There is also an artificial tortoise cave constructed nearby, which is interesting to explore and good for photos.

The entire area is peaceful, clean, and very well maintained. The strong wind, fresh air, and wide-open landscape give a sense of calm and relaxation. This place is perfect for nature lovers, photographers, couples, families, and adventure seekers.

Overall, Amappara is a must-visit destination in Ramakkalmedu.
The combination of jeep safari, sunset view, scenic beauty, and the unique tortoise cave makes it a truly unforgettable experience.
Highly recommended for anyone visiting Idukki!

Deer Park in Thenmala is one of those places where nature truly meets serenity. Nestled amidst lush greenery and gentle hills, the park offers a peaceful escape from the rush of city life. The entry area is well maintained, with clean walkways, shaded resting spots, and clear signboards guiding visitors through the park. The forest surroundings make it an ideal stop for families, couples, and nature enthusiasts who love calm, scenic environments.

The main attraction — the herd of spotted deer — is delightful to watch. They move freely within a spacious, natural habitat, and visitors can observe them up close from safe viewing areas. The park staff are friendly and ensure both the animals and visitors are well cared for. There’s a light breeze, birds chirping, and the soft sound of the forest — it all adds to the relaxing experience. Kids especially love feeding the deer (with permitted food), making it a great educational and joyful outing.

Overall, Deer Park at Thenmala is not just about seeing animals — it’s about feeling connected to nature. The calm atmosphere, greenery, and mountain backdrop make it a perfect place to unwind and click beautiful pictures. Combined with nearby attractions like the dam and adventure zone, it’s a must-visit spot in Thenmala for a refreshing day trip. 🌿🦌✨

Panpoli Park is a public children's park located in Panpoli, near Tenkasi, Tamil Nadu, known for its serene environment and basic amenities like slides, swings, and picnic tables.
Location & Information
Address: Panpoli Park, 26FX+XFC, Panboli, Tamil Nadu 627807. You can view its location on Google Maps.
Hours: The park is generally listed as being open 24 hours every day, but this is a general listing and visitors should verify locally for specific operational hours.

Atmosphere: Reviewers describe the park as a clean, well-maintained place with good weather and a calm, peaceful atmosphere.

We visited this temple on a Full Moon Saturday and had a truly memorable experience. Our group, along with a family from Pondicherry, were the only visitors that day. The entry fee was ₹30 per person, with an additional ₹50 for camera phones. Since we were unfamiliar with the route, the forest ranger kindly arranged for a local guide to accompany us.

The path to the temple led us along the banks of the Kadana Nathi Dam and into the dense forest of the Papanasam Reserve Forest. Along the way, there was a river crossing where visitors could take a refreshing bath. After the river crossing, the path became steep, providing a good test of fitness. We reached the temple in about 45-50 minutes.

The temple and the surrounding forest were incredibly serene, offering a sense of tranquility that is hard to describe. It was also quite windy, which added a refreshing feel to the whole experience. We drank water from a nearby stream and spotted various kinds of birds, including many peacocks, as well as monkeys and boars. The massive, ancient trees surrounding the temple added to its majestic atmosphere. The temple itself was awe-inspiring, and there was a pond with fish that was delightful to watch.

We also visited a nearby mosque and its associated cave. We spent about an hour relaxing, inhaling the clean air, and soaking in the natural beauty of the place. The family we met kindly shared snacks like Medu Vada and biscuits with us. On the way back, the local guide took us on a different path along the sandy banks of the river leading to the dam, which was an incredible experience. The guide was very friendly and supportive throughout the journey.

Overall, it was a wonderful visit filled with natural beauty, wildlife, and kind-hearted people. We highly recommend it to anyone looking for a peaceful and rejuvenating outing.

Kottavasal View Point is a wonderful scenic spot that offers a breathtaking panoramic view from the top. From here, we can clearly see the beautiful Tenkasi area, with its greenery, hills, and vast landscapes spreading out below. The view is especially refreshing and peaceful, making it a great place to stop and enjoy nature.

One of the best advantages of this viewpoint is its easy accessibility. It is located very close to the famous 13 Arch Bridge, so visitors can conveniently cover both places in a single trip. Moreover, the viewpoint is right beside the road, which makes it perfect for travelers who want to take a short break, capture some photos

"A hidden gem offering panoramic views of lush green valleys and rolling hills. Ideal for early morning visits to witness vibrant skies and tranquil surroundings. A peaceful spot for nature enthusiasts and photographers."

A scenic viewpoint located near the Kerala Tamil Nadu border in the Kottavasal ghat section, offering wide views of forested hills, valleys, and the winding railway line below. The area feels calm and refreshing, especially during early morning and post monsoon seasons.

Access is limited and there are no proper tourist facilities, so it is best suited for short stops and nature viewing rather than long visits. The viewpoint is more popular among railway and nature enthusiasts than regular tourists.

Pros
Beautiful valley and hill views. Peaceful and less crowded. Great for nature lovers.

Cons
No proper facilities. Limited road access. Not well developed as a tourist spot.

Shabarimala visit in 2018 after August will be a different experience for ever. After the August floods, the entire place has become very unstable. Pampa River water has disappeared weeks after the heavy rains. The phenomenon which is so overwhelming to see and feel. The silt from the upriver has landed right at the pampa base camp area where thousands of Ayyappa devotees used to take holy dip before climbing the Shabarimala mountain. The inefficiency of the kerala govt and the Devosvam board is clearly seen at the site. Even after a month of flood , except for few Earthmovers removing the silt from the river bed, no other cleaning and development work has commended. Except for asking for donations and sympathy from the rest of the world, no concrete action has been initiated at the ground. So support or helplines for the pilgrims, no proper arrangements of water and sanitation near pampa. It's heartbreaking to see the surrounding place being devastated by floods caused by human  mistakes. Only time will tell what we learn from our mistakes and how we safeguard this mother earth.

We went on a 5-day girls’ trip to Rajasthan, and we enjoyed it a lot — loads and loads of fun and so many beautiful memories! The itinerary was very well planned, covering Jodhpur, Jaipur, and Jaisalmer. Everything felt very convenient  booking and coordination were so easy, and the trip brought back so many wonderful memories. Overall, this trip was a perfect blend of adventure and comfort.
Special mention to Bhagawan bro — he was amazing and guided us throughout. He took care of everything, was very friendly, and managed time so well, which made the trip really easy for us. He also ensured we got the full five days of sightseeing until each of us was dropped off.
Thanks to Exoticamp for such a wonderful experience!

This 5 days Rajasthan trip was the best decision I made, even though I was very skeptical. The itinerary is very well planned, jodhpur itinerary could be better though. This was my first camping experience with Exoticamp, and it turned out to be an absolutely amazing one! We loved every single moment of it. Initially, I was quite worried since I was traveling with my 3-year-old kid — I wasn’t sure if I had made the right decision. But from the moment we arrived, all my doubts disappeared.
We came to know that we were the only family booked for this trip, which meant we had the entire place to ourselves — right in the middle of the beautiful mangrove forest! Even though we were the only guests, the Exoticamp team never compromised on their service. In fact, they went above and beyond to make sure we were comfortable and had everything we needed.
The place was super clean, well-maintained, and beautifully set up. The hosts made sure we were comfortable at all times, provided food on time, and even took care of my baby’s needs with so much care and attention.
One of the highlights of our trip was the kayaking session — it was simply awesome! Paddling through the calm mangrove waterways surrounded by nature was such a peaceful and thrilling experience.
Thank you, Exoticamp, for such a wonderful experience — we can’t wait to camp with you again!

Papanasam's Paana Theertham Waterfalls, located in the Kalakad-Mundanthurai Tiger Reserve (KMTR), is a majestic waterfall cascading from the Western Ghats. Surrounded by dense forests and pristine nature, it is a hidden gem offering breathtaking views. The falls hold spiritual significance and are considered sacred by locals. However, access to the falls is restricted, and visitors can only visit with prior permission from the forest department. This ensures the preservation of its natural beauty and ecosystem.

Highlights:

Variety of adventure activities, including rock climbing, zip lining, and rappelling
Stunning natural surroundings, with lush forests and picturesque valleys
Well-trained and safety-conscious staff
Clean and well-maintained facilities
Perfect spot for team-building exercises and corporate events

A must-visit for:

Adventure enthusiasts seeking a thrilling experience
Nature lovers who want to explore the great outdoors
Families looking for a fun and exciting day out
Corporate teams seeking a unique team-building experience

Tips:

Wear comfortable and sturdy shoes for adventure activities
Bring plenty of water and snacks to stay hydrated and energized
Follow safety instructions carefully to ensure a safe experience
Take your time to enjoy the stunning natural surroundings

Thenmala Adventure Park is an absolute must-visit destination for anyone seeking an adrenaline rush amidst nature. The park's safety-conscious staff, stunning natural surroundings, and variety of adventure activities make it an unforgettable experience. Highly recommended!"

Please be careful while visiting this place. One of my friends recently visited this park and there was an activity that was apparently not open for public - but there were no sign boards informing that. My friend climbed all the way up and then the officials suddenly decided that he should be asked to return down. As he climbed back he fell on to a concrete slab with absolutely no layer of protection on the floor. He had a spine fracture and underwent an immediate surgery that night at Kim’s. He is currently recovering with assistance and will definitely take a couple of months to reach normalcy. If this is the value placed on human life by our government spaces then where exactly can we safely enjoy a holiday ?

The park offers a zipline, multiple obstacle courses, paddle boarding, a small shooting range, and more.

To access the park, you need to buy an entry ticket at the dam’s entry point and then travel about 1 km to reach the location. There’s a parking area where you can leave your vehicle, and if you’re coming as a group, you can easily spend 2 to 3 hours enjoying the activities.

The guides here are more helpful and organized compared to most government-run parks, which adds to the overall experience.

Visited Mottai Dam last weekend and was pleasantly surprised! It’s a quiet, scenic spot surrounded by hills and greenery—perfect if you're looking to relax away from the crowd. The place isn't commercialized, which makes it feel peaceful and untouched.

We enjoyed walking around the dam area and soaking in the natural beauty. No official boating or major tourist setup, but some locals offer jeep rides to nearby waterfalls. Definitely recommend checking out the private falls nearby if you're up for a bit of adventure.

It commands a spectacular panoramic view of the Western Ghats. It is home to lush paddy fields, coconut palms and orchards of other tropical fruits. It is here that the mountain top and the clouds merge into one. It receives both the monsoons of India. It is one of Tamil Nadu’s prettiest villages. I am talking about Mekkarai, the surreal village located on the border of Tamil Nadu Kerala and that is part of the ecosystem that connects Shendurney wildlife sanctuary with Konni reserve forest.
Imagine this view…lush green or golden paddy fields (depending on maturity of crop) swaying in the winds that are blowing from the west, a consistent drizzle in the air, dense jungle views in the yonder and dark monsoon clouds kissing the mountain top. This is the view that nature presents to you as soon as you enter the tiny village of Mekkarai that is located in the Tenkasi district of Tamil Nadu. In the middle of the village lie the homes and the paddy fields around them, towards the mountain side are mountain streams, waterfalls and umpteen scenic locations and to one side of the village lies the Adavinainar dam that commands stunning views of the surrounding Western Ghat mountains.

Mekkarai View Point looks like a living painting, with breathtaking scenic views all around. The lush greenery, hills, and mist together create a calm, picture-perfect spot that feels truly refreshing and serene.

The Adavinainar Dam near Tenkasi is a beautiful and serene spot perfect for a quiet nature break. I recently had the pleasure of visiting Mekkarai Village, and it truly left a lasting impression. Nestled in the heart of nature, this charming village showcases the beauty of sustainable agriculture at its finest. The lush green fields, meticulously cared for by the local farmers, paint a picturesque landscape that is both calming and inspiring. The dedication and hard work of the villagers are evident in every corner, reflecting a deep respect for their land and traditions. Mekkarai Village isn’t just a place; it’s an experience that beautifully connects you with the essence of rural life and the art of farming. If you’re looking for a serene getaway with a genuine touch of agricultural heritage, Mekkarai is the perfect destination.

Tenkasi is the second largest town of  Tirunelveli District, Tamil Nadu on the Madurai - Kollam highway.Situated on the foothills of the Western Ghats,Tenkasi’s natural beauty and scenic locales make it a great holiday destination.River Chittar flows through this picturesque destination.

This town is famous for its light monsoon showers, one of the most enjoyable experiences in this city.This city is called the Kasi of the South with reference to the beautiful and old Kasi Vishwanathar Temple situated here.Apart from this temple, Tenkasi has many waterfalls where a person can spend some time in leisure.This waterfalls add to the lush green backdrops and the soothing tunes of the drizzles, luring many a tourist from the world over.

Travelling in Tenkasi is not much of a task as it is easy to rent a taxi,auto-rickshaws or hire a car.

Anytime between May and August is the best time to visit the city&enjoy it to the fullest as it is the perfect place to let loose,relax and find peace.

Beautiful Village.

The present Tenkasi district was a part of erstwhile Tirunelveli district. Tenkasi district was formed in 2019 as per G.O. No 427 dated 12.11.2019.Tenkasi shares its boundaries with Virudhunagar in the North, Thoothukudi in the East, Tirunelveli in the South and Kerala in the West. The district is nourished by two major rivers namely Chittar and Anumanadhi, because of which agriculture flourishes in this area. More than 65% of the population is engaged in agriculture and related activities. Water sources such as Gundar, Adavinayinar, Karupanadhi and Ramanadhi dam along with more than 800 tanks succour irrigation.
